item_a.name: Dyson V11 Torque Drive
item_a.image_alt: Dyson V11 Torque Drive cordless stick vacuum with LCD screen and cleaning attachments
pros: Powerful 185 AW suction with High Torque cleaner head
Up to 60 minutes of runtime in Eco mode
Dynamic Load Sensor automatically adjusts power between carpet and hard floors
LCD screen displays performance in real-time
cons: Heavier at 6.68 lbs compared to newer models
Older filtration system captures 99.97% of particles but not as advanced as V15
item_a.best_for: Budget-conscious buyers who want premium Dyson performance without paying for the latest technology innovations.

item_b.name: Dyson V15 Detect
item_b.image_alt: Dyson V15 Detect cordless vacuum with green laser and LCD particle counter display
pros: Laser Slim Fluffy cleaner head reveals microscopic dust on hard floors
Piezo sensor counts and categorizes particles in real-time on LCD
230 AW suction power, 24% more than V11
Advanced whole-machine HEPA filtration captures 99.99% of particles down to 0.3 microns
item_b.best_for: Allergy sufferers and tech enthusiasts who want scientific proof of cleaning performance and the most advanced dust detection available.

comparison_summary: The Dyson V15 Detect represents a significant technological leap with its laser dust detection and particle counting sensor, delivering 230 AW of suction compared to the V11's 185 AW. However, the V11 offers 85% of the performance at roughly 25-30% less cost, making it an excellent value for those who don't need scientific dust visualization. For most households, the V11 provides exceptional cleaning power, while the V15 is ideal for allergy sufferers and data-driven cleaners.
verdict_text: The Dyson V15 Detect wins for its superior suction power, advanced filtration, and innovative laser detection technology that justifies the premium for allergy sufferers and tech enthusiasts.

key_differences.item_a: Visual cleaning only, no laser technology
key_differences.item_b: Green laser reveals microscopic dust + piezo sensor counts particles

key_differences.item_a: Advanced whole-machine filtration, captures 99.97% of particles
key_differences.item_b: Whole-machine HEPA filtration, captures 99.99% down to 0.3 microns

key_differences.item_a: Up to 60 minutes in Eco mode
key_differences.item_b: Up to 60 minutes in Eco mode

key_differences.item_a: Shows power mode, runtime, and maintenance alerts
key_differences.item_b: Shows power mode, runtime, maintenance alerts, plus real-time particle count and size distribution

key_differences.item_a: High Torque cleaner head with Dynamic Load Sensor
key_differences.item_b: Laser Slim Fluffy head for hard floors + High Torque head with anti-tangle technology

seo_content:
When comparing the Dyson V11 vs V15, you're looking at two generations of Dyson's premium cordless vacuum technology, separated by significant innovations in dust detection and filtration. The Dyson V15 Detect, released in 2021, introduced groundbreaking features like laser dust illumination and real-time particle counting, while the V11 Torque Drive from 2019 remains a powerful and more affordable option that still outperforms most competitors.

The most striking difference in the V15 vs V11 debate is the V15's Laser Slim Fluffy cleaner head, which uses a precisely-angled green laser to illuminate microscopic dust particles on hard floors that are otherwise invisible to the naked eye. This isn't just a gimmick—it genuinely reveals surprising amounts of dust that you'd miss without it. The V15 also features a piezo acoustic sensor that counts and sizes particles up to 15,000 times per second, displaying the data on an upgraded LCD screen. This scientific approach to cleaning provides proof of performance and can be particularly reassuring for allergy sufferers who need confidence in their vacuum's effectiveness.

In terms of raw power, the Dyson V15 delivers 230 Air Watts of suction compared to the V11's 185 AW—a 24% increase that translates to noticeably better pickup on carpets and more effective deep cleaning. The V15 also features improved whole-machine HEPA filtration that captures 99.99% of particles down to 0.3 microns, compared to the V11's already-impressive 99.97% rating. For households with severe allergies, asthma, or pets, this enhanced filtration combined with the particle sensor makes the V15 a compelling choice.

However, the Dyson V11 shouldn't be dismissed. At $449-$599, it costs $150-$200 less than the V15 while still delivering exceptional performance that surpasses most cordless vacuums on the market. The V11's Dynamic Load Sensor technology automatically adjusts suction between carpet and hard floors, and its 60-minute runtime matches the V15 despite being an older model. The V11 is also slightly lighter at 6.68 lbs versus 6.83 lbs, which matters during extended cleaning sessions.

For value-conscious buyers, the V11 represents outstanding bang-for-buck, delivering premium Dyson engineering without paying for the latest innovations. But if you prioritize having the absolute best filtration, want scientific proof of cleaning performance, or find the laser detection genuinely useful for your hard floors, the V15's premium is justified. Both vacuums share excellent build quality, versatile attachments, and the convenience of cordless operation that has made Dyson the category leader.

faq.q: Is the Dyson V15 worth the extra money over the V11?
faq.a: The V15 is worth the extra $150-$200 if you have allergies, want the best possible filtration (99.99% vs 99.97%), or find value in the laser dust detection and particle counting features. For general cleaning needs, the V11 offers excellent performance at better value.

faq.q: Does the Dyson V15 have better suction than the V11?
faq.a: Yes, the V15 delivers 230 Air Watts of suction compared to the V11's 185 AW, representing a 24% increase in suction power. This translates to better deep carpet cleaning and more effective particle pickup.

faq.q: How long do the batteries last on the V11 vs V15?
faq.a: Both the V11 and V15 offer up to 60 minutes of runtime in Eco mode with non-motorized attachments. In more powerful modes with motorized heads, expect 20-30 minutes for both models. The runtime performance is essentially identical.

faq.q: Is the laser on the Dyson V15 actually useful?
faq.a: The green laser on the V15 is genuinely useful on hard floors, revealing dust particles that are invisible under normal lighting. Many users report being surprised by how much dust they were missing. However, it only works on the Laser Slim Fluffy head designed for hard floors, not on carpet attachments.

faq.q: Which is better for pet hair, the Dyson V11 or V15?
faq.a: The V15 has a slight edge for pet hair due to its stronger 230 AW suction and anti-tangle technology in the High Torque head. However, the V11 is also excellent for pet hair with its 185 AW suction and de-tangling comb design. Both handle pet hair effectively, so the V11 offers better value unless you need the V15's superior filtration for pet allergies.

meta_title: Dyson V11 vs V15 Detect (2026): Which Should You Buy?
meta_description: Detailed comparison of Dyson V11 vs V15 Detect. We break down suction power, laser detection, filtration, battery life, and value to help you pick the right cordless vacuum.
